_meta.source_note: Seeded from NVD, CISA KEV, and vendor advisories through mid-2025. Validate against live feeds before publishing.

vendors.name: CrowdStrike
vendors.wr_take: Best patch velocity in endpoint. July 2024 was an ops failure not a breach — but 8.5M machines proved dangerous concentration.

cve_list.description: Falcon sensor privilege escalation via kernel driver race condition
Falcon console stored XSS via malformed alert description field
Falcon Data Replicator S3 path traversal — read-only adjacent tenant data
Agent local privilege escalation on Windows via unquoted service path
Management console SSRF via webhook configuration endpoint

vendors.name: Ivanti
vendors.wr_take: Remediation pace is structural not situational. 16 active KEVs is not bad luck — it is product lifecycle debt that marketing cannot patch.

cve_list.description: Connect Secure stack buffer overflow — zero-day exploited by UNC5337 (PRC) before disclosure
Connect Secure local privilege escalation chained with CVE-2025-0282
EPMM command injection — required emergency repatch after incomplete initial fix
Microsoft Defender for Endpoint spoofing via crafted file
Windows TCP/IP RCE exploitable without authentication
PAN-OS auth bypass in management interface — exploited in the wild pre-patch
GlobalProtect gateway RCE via malformed handshake packet
Panorama SQL injection via device group filter parameter

vendors.name: Fortinet
vendors.wr_take: FortiOS has been a persistent KEV fixture for three years. At some point this stops being bad luck and starts being architecture.

cve_list.description: FortiOS SSL-VPN out-of-bounds write — unauthenticated RCE exploited by multiple threat actors
FortiOS format string vuln in fgfmd daemon — unauthenticated RCE
FortiAP stack-based buffer overflow in CLI command handler
NX-OS CLI command injection — root RCE exploited by Velvet Ant (PRC-nexus)
ASA/FTD management tunnel DoS — part of ArcaneDoor PRC campaign
ASA persistent backdoor via crafted VPN client package
Security Gateway path traversal enabling credential extraction — exploited ITW
Quantum Gateway local privilege escalation via kernel module
Junos OS SRX/EX unauthenticated RCE via J-Web — exploited ITW
Junos OS Routing Protocol Daemon DoS via malformed BGP update
NetScaler ADC/Gateway unauthenticated RCE — mass exploitation by ransomware groups
Virtual Apps and Desktops privilege escalation via session recording component
CloudFormation SSRF via template URL parameter allowing metadata access
Windows Update rollback enabling reintroduction of patched flaws — exploited ITW
Hyper-V elevation of privilege — zero-day exploited before Patch Tuesday
DWM Core Library privilege escalation used in QakBot post-exploitation

vendors.name: Broadcom / VMware
vendors.wr_take: Post-acquisition patching velocity has degraded. vCenter and ESXi remain tier-1 ransomware targets. The Broadcom integration has not helped.

cve_list.description: vCenter Server heap overflow in DCERPC — unauthenticated RCE
vCenter privilege escalation to root chained with CVE-2024-38812
Workstation/Fusion XHCI USB use-after-free enabling VM escape

vendors.name: Okta
vendors.wr_take: 2023 breach found via customer report not internal detection — but disclosure and recovery were textbook. RRR tells the nuanced story.

cve_list.description: Classic Engine session token fixation allowing account takeover without MFA
On-prem AD agent authentication bypass — legacy agent versions only
SharePoint deserialization RCE with Entra auth bypass chain
Privilege Cloud session token insufficient validation allowing privilege escalation

vendors.name: BeyondTrust
vendors.wr_take: December 2024 breach via compromised API key hit US Treasury. Detection was external. The PAM vendor getting PAMed is a reputational category event.

cve_list.description: Privileged Remote Access command injection — exploited in Treasury breach Dec 2024
Password Safe OS command injection requiring existing admin credentials
Enterprise path traversal on Windows — unauthenticated arbitrary file read
Enterprise RCE via insecure session storage deserialization — requires auth

vendors.name: SolarWinds
vendors.wr_take: Meaningful improvement since SUNBURST. Faster patches, better disclosure. Still carries institutional trust debt procurement teams have not fully priced in.

cve_list.description: Web Help Desk Java deserialization RCE — unauthenticated, exploited ITW
Web Help Desk hardcoded credential allowing unauthorized database access
Nessus path traversal allowing authenticated read of arbitrary server files
InsightVM stored XSS via asset tag field allowing session hijack

vendors.name: Cloudflare
vendors.wr_take: Detected the Okta-linked intrusion in 23 minutes, self-contained it, then published one of the most detailed public incident reports ever written. This is what React A looks like.

cve_list.description: Workers sandbox escape via crafted WebAssembly module — no confirmed customer impact
Identity Cloud SSRF via OAuth redirect parameter
ZIA DLP bypass via chunked transfer encoding evasion
Actions OIDC token leakage via workflow log output in specific configurations
Confluence RCE via malicious macro — exploited ITW in ransomware campaigns
Confluence template injection RCE — unauthenticated, mass exploitation into 2024

vendors.name: Veeam
vendors.wr_take: Most targeted backup solution by ransomware operators. Compromising backup before encryption is the playbook. Veeam exposure is existential risk for recovery.

cve_list.description: Backup & Replication deserialization RCE — unauthenticated, exploited by ransomware groups
Backup Enterprise Manager auth bypass allowing full console access
Jelly template injection enabling unauthenticated RCE — exploited ITW
File read via path traversal in mid-server component
NetWeaver AS ABAP OS command injection via report execution — exploited ITW
BusinessObjects information disclosure via crafted BI Launchpad request
EcoStruxure Panel Server unauthenticated path traversal — config file exfiltration
Modicon M340 PLC hardcoded credentials in web server component
SINEC NMS SQL injection via network management interface — unauthenticated
SIMATIC S7-1500 DoS via crafted Profinet IO — safety system impact possible
MyChart SSRF via patient portal document upload functionality

vendors.name: Change Healthcare
vendors.wr_take: Feb 2024 breach disrupted US healthcare payments for weeks. Ransom paid. Second group hit the same systems weeks later. 15B transactions/year. Concentration risk was hiding in plain sight.

cve_list.description: Citrix NetScaler vuln used as initial access in February 2024 UnitedHealth breach
Citrix Bleed session token extraction — entry point in Change Healthcare compromise
PolicyCenter IDOR allowing cross-tenant policy data access
Email Security Gateway XSS via malformed MIME boundary in quarantine interface

vendors.name: Barracuda Networks
vendors.wr_take: May 2023 zero-day required full hardware replacement — not patching, replacement. That is a category of incident severity most boards have no framework for.

cve_list.description: ESG appliance RCE via malformed TAR attachment — required full hardware replacement
Email Security Gateway auth bypass via crafted LDAP authentication request
Alliance Gateway message manipulation via unauthenticated API endpoint
Experience Cloud guest user SOQL injection allowing data enumeration
HCM SSRF via integration connector URL parameter
Platform stored XSS via asset tag field in industrial asset inventory
IdentityIQ improper access control — HTTP access to protected static content
IdentityIQ incorrect content type XSS
Improper input validation in EOL OVA Connect component

vendors.name: Commvault
vendors.wr_take: Commvault patched CVE-2025-34028 (CVSS 10) in 3 days. But active KEV exploitation and a nation-state breach via CVE-2025-3928 suggest detection lagged behind the patch velocity.

cve_list.description: Command Center path traversal RCE — pre-auth remote code execution via ZIP upload
Web Server vuln exploited by nation-state for Azure environment breach March 2025
CommServe argument injection — unauthenticated RCE via qlogin manipulation
Wiz platform security vulnerability
Google Cloud security infrastructure vulnerability
Netskope Client static token auth allowing unauthorized enrollment and impersonation
Netskope Client config tampering via MITM
PingAM Java Policy Agent path traversal — file manipulation and data exfiltration without user interaction
PingFederate Google Adapter thread exhaustion via non-200 responses
